name: Snoop ‘ Tog - Drop it like it's hot|Deck Guide
description_md: # **Darrell snoops around to capture _Evidence_ on his [Note Book](/card/09045) and his real [Tog](https://postimg.cc/v4WhZjdg) [Material](/card/09016)** `Please don't forget to press the ♥ if you like the write-up and want to say thanks :)` [![Scarlet-Keys.png](https://i.postimg.cc/mDwfXyQg/Scarlet-Keys.png)](https://postimg.cc/CBzQdDP3) Credit: Fantasy Flight Games ___ ### **Introduction:** Now that the Scarlet Keys - Investigator Expansion has hit the table, it's time for some deck guides with the new investigators! This deck is meant for multiplayer, with at least one other investigator dedicated to enemy management. The number of variables may be overwhelming for beginners, requiring you to pay attention to your _Evidence_, keep track of the ones on cards in play, be aware of what is in your discard pile, ready exhausted assets, take additional actions, move automatically when you empty a location and drop clues. **For that very reason, this is one of the most fun decks I have ever played, and it never gets monotonous! ** `Disclaimer:` This deck guide was created before the Campaign Expansion was released. Some cards and assumptions in this guide may be made irrelevant later on. I will keep it updated with a changelog if that is the case. ___ ### **Table of Contents:** * Overview * Main Strategy * Drop Clues Forwards * Collecting _Evidence_ * Customizable Cards * Scavenging * Other Cards * Upgrade Path * Make Your Own Deck! ___ ### **Overview:** || | |-|-| |Difficulty:||★★★★☆ | Enemy Management:||★★☆☆☆ | Clue-getting:||★★★★★ | Encounter protection:||★★★★☆ | Survivability:||★★★☆☆ | Economy:|| ★★★☆☆ | Card Drawing:||★★★★☆ ___ ### **Main Strategy:** This deck is centred around 2 main mechanics: * The newly improved clue-dropping for double benefits: the direct effect obtained while dropping one or more clues and (ab)using the compensation tools introduced in this box: [Research Notes](/card/09045) and [Press Pass](/card/09055). * The well known [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) that plays your _Items_ and let you commit [Newspaper (2)](/card/03313) and [The Raven Quill](/card/09042) for 2 and get them back to your hand. ___ ### **Drop Clues Forwards:** This deck takes full advantage of the clue-dropping mechanic introduced in Dunwich and is improved in TSK. Dropping clues used to feel _wrong_. Gaining a clue was not only complicated and time-consuming, but it was also using cards and resources. Placing them back on your location felt like a step backwards toward the game's resolution. Let's see how this changed now: ** Benefits of Dropping Clues:** The clue-dropping cards compensate for the loss in tempo with a pretty powerful effect: * [Captivating Discovery](/card/09047) makes you draw 2 cards per clue dropped. In this deck, we benefit from dropping a lot of clues, so you will often draw 4 to 6 cards and drop 2 to 3 clues based on if you find a weakness and if you would rather wait to draw it or not. Choosing when you draw your weakness is a powerful effect and should not be underestimated. * [Dr. William T. Maleson](/card/02302) was always the #1 Meat shield in the game. Let's now use him for his other benefit: re-drawing Encounter cards you don't want to see. If you are playing with 2 or more players, you will most likely be paired with another investigator dedicated to enemy management. * Shuffling enemies back is a great way to stream them towards your fighter while taking your share of treacheries. You can also use [William](/card/02302)'s capacity for filtering out tests that would threaten you if [Ruined Film](/card/09017) seriously depleted your sanity * The upgraded version is icing on the cake: protect your team **at any location**, choose between 2 cards instead of 1, and most importantly, discard the encounter card that you didn't select, potentially getting rid of _really_ annoying cards like [Ancient Evils](/card/01166) or [Beyond the Veil](/card/02084). * [Quick Study](/card/04154) was niche, but it can finally shine now. It allows you to drop a clue to succeed at more or less any test, thanks to the +3. With the [Research Notes](/card/09045), you are also granted 1 _Evidence_ that you can turn immediately into a +2 with [Darrell's](/card/09015) ability. With this +5 at any stat, [Darrell Simmons](/card/09015) can break any test the scenario throws at him. * [Analysis](/card/09049) completes the set. This seemingly weaker card actually has the highest clue-dropping capability and can be committed to any test. * This can be used to secure success on a critical test if your clues do not matter so much anymore, like during a fight with an Elite enemy. * Alternatively, you can use it on a teammate that would like to fish certain tokens like / for instance. * [Forewarned](/card/03150) was an option, but unfortunately, only being able to drop 1 clue for an effect that is eventually not so critical since [Quick Study](/card/04154) can help us pass almost any test and [Dr. William T. Maleson](/card/02302) sorts the annoying cards like [Ancient Evils](/card/01166). ** Double the Gain: ** This deck can drop a significant number of clues each turn, which is the fuel for our new shiny toys, turning each dropped clue into a potentially **huge** profit: * [Newspaper (2)](/card/03313) rewards you for dropping your last clue by giving it back to you the next time you discover a clue. This means that it works with the ability of [Research Notes](/card/09045)! * Since it is not unique, you can have 2 copies in play, increasing the speed at which you collect back your dropped clues. * Note that [Newspaper (2)](/card/03313) does not exhaust, and thus, it can be used multiple times per turn! * [Research Notes](/card/09045) collects 1 _Evidence_ per dropped clue, and since **it is not unique**, you can have 2 in play. **This means that with each clue dropped, you gain 2 _Evidence_** (1 on each copy) that you can use to get clues later. * The best way to use the [Research Notes](/card/09045) is to drop clues on lower shroud locations to accumulate _Evidence_ and collect them back easily. Then spend the _Evidence_ on a high-shroud location to test (0) and gain clues easily. * Another positive thing about [Research Notes](/card/09045) is that you can drop clues at a non-VP location and then use the _Evidence_ to easily empty locations with Victory Points. * [Press Pass](/card/09055) gives you an additional action if you dropped a clue during your turn. With this deck, given a few setups turn at worst, it should be possible every turn. This virtually makes [Captivating Discovery](/card/09047) or compensates for the action spent on [Research Notes](/card/09045) to get the clues back. What's best is that **it is not unique**, so for the additional price of a [Relic Hunter](/card/01695), you can get a second additional action per turn! ** Synergies:** * You can drop a clue with [Quick Study](/card/04154) to increase your while using the on [Research Notes](/card/09045). This combo dramatically increases the chances of spending all your _Evidence_ stored on one copy in a single test, while collecting an additional _Evidence_ on your second copy! * Same goes for [Analysis](/card/09049), which can help you to succeed by a significant number while getting all the clues dropped back. * [Knowledge is Power](/card/05231) gives an huge burst in tempo by allowing you to perform the ability on the [Research Notes](/card/09045) without spending the action **and any _Evidence_** since it ignores _all_ costs. As this is considered on the edge of how [Knowledge is Power](/card/05231) should be used, I only used 1 copy of [Knowledge is Power](/card/05231) not to break the game completely. If you do not mind, then go for a 2nd one ASAP; if you do, remove the one copy. ** Conclusion:** Once the engine is running, this deck will allow you to gain various benefits out of dropping clues: collecting _Evidence_, drawing loads of cards, guaranteeing success to a test, choosing your Encounter card or boosting a skill value. * All the _Evidence_ collected will then translate into even more cards with [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041), lower test difficulty with [Darrell's](/card/09015) ability and easy clues no matter the shroud value. Icing on the cake: you will do all of this without spending any of your 3 basic actions, thanks to [Press Pass](/card/09055). ___ ### **Collecting _Evidence_:** As discussed in the earlier section, the clue-dropping mechanic will generate an insane number of _Evidence_. Let's review where: * [Darrell's Kodak](/card/09016) is in play from the beginning of the game. Mainly thanks to [Dr. William T. Maleson](/card/02302), you can sort out the Encounter card that you draw and keep only the ones that would enable you to collect an _Evidence_ on the [Darrell's Kodak](/card/09016) and that you feel confident enough you can collect before it is gone. For instance, if you draw an enemy and want your fighter to kill it before your turn so you can investigate, you will not be able to collect the _Evidence_ on it. So be smart! * [Research Notes](/card/09045), as explained earlier, will collect 1 _Evidence_ *per clue dropped* and *per copy* on the table. * [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041) will also collect _Evidence_. regularly as it is highly likely that you will succeed by 3 or more at least once a turn, especially while testing on the [Research Notes](/card/09045). * While you are likely to play most of your _Evidence_ on the turn when you get them, you can still collect and store a high number up front to prepare for fast cycling of your deck and therefore seeing your [Ruined Film](/card/09017) often. ___ ### **Scavenging:** As every good Deck, [Scavenging (0)](/card/01073) and [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) bring a fantastic value in 2 different ways: ** Fast setup:** * [Short Supply](/card/08071) place a lot of your _Items_ in your discard pile from the beginning of the game. Play [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) to play them as soon as you succeed an investigation by 2 or more (which should happen every turn). [Scavenging (0)](/card/01073) is already good to tutor your Items. * [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) and [Arcane Enlightenment](/card/60205) is your priority for Mulligan, and you shouldn't feel bad doing a hard mulligan for it. If it turns out that both copies go to your discard pile because of [Short Supply](/card/08071), you are still very likely to get at least one back with [Resourceful](/card/03039). If not, well... cycle fast with [Captivating Discovery](/card/09047). * Some cards are more annoying than others if discarded by [Short Supply](/card/08071) since we cannot get them back with this deck: besides [Quick Study](/card/04154) and [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041), I want to emphasise on [Arcane Enlightenment](/card/60205). This is your second priority for the mulligan, and you should not shuffle it back. which will prevent you from having 2 [Newspaper (2)](/card/03313) in play. If it is a victim of [Short Supply](/card/08071), you will not be able to play both [Research Notes](/card/09045) before you cycle your deck once. * To play items from your hand , commit them to tests and then Scavenge them into play directly. Except for the [Hiking Boots](/card/08035) and [Press Pass](/card/09055), they conveniently all commit for tests. * The [Schoffner's Catalogue](/card/08072) is here to pay for your _Items_ since you can cycle it. Note that it says _Item assets_ and therefore cannot pay for [The Raven Quill](/card/09042), unfortunately. ** Higher value: ** [Newspaper (2)](/card/03313) and [The Raven Quill](/card/09042) are both _Items_ that commit for , which is fantastic to play them in a loop to oversucceed. This works before you even upgrade your [Scavenging](/card/01073). ___ ### **Customizable Cards:** **[The Raven Quill](/card/09042):** * [The Raven Quill](/card/09042) is another cornerstone of this deck. You name the [Research Notes](/card/09045) and let it tick its own boxes at the end of each scenario. Before you have the [Research Notes](/card/09045) in play, you can loop it with [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) to commit it for and return it to your hand. * For the upgrades, we will choose the _Spectral Binding_ first, for 1xp, making the one copy of [Research Notes](/card/09045) not take any slot and therefore making it possible to replace the second copy of [Arcane Enlightenment](/card/60205) and still have 4 hand slots. * As the [The Raven Quill](/card/09042) gains more experience, we can choose _Interwoven Ink_, which readies another asset when we use the ability of the attached [Research Notes](/card/09045). The assets that we can ready this way are **[Press Pass](/card/09055)** for another additional action during your turn and more clue-dropping shenanigans, **[Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041)** to collect another _Evidence_ during the same test, [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) if you still have some setup to do, [Hiking Boots](/card/08035) or [Quick Study](/card/04154) in more specific situations. ** [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041):** You do not really need to tick any box on this card to get value, but as you run out of upgrades for your deck, there are 2 that can be interesting: * _Peer review_: if you do not need more card draw and want to support your team, this makes it more likely for you to gain an _Evidence_ and get the most out of it by offering others to draw cards. * _Research Grant_: depending on what your team needs the most, being able to help your teammates with paying for their cards can be extremely useful, especially in the early scenario and late campaign when cards tend to become more expensive. ** The interaction of the two:** To clarify the way [The Raven Quill](/card/09042) - _Interwoven Ink_ works with [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041), here is the sequence: * Use the on [Research Notes](/card/09045) with [The Raven Quill](/card/09042) attached. * Succeed by 3 or more and exhaust [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041) to collect an _Evidence_ on it. * After resolving the test, _Interwoven Ink_ will ready [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041), which is then ready to use again for another _Evidence_. * Exhaust [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041) immediately to collect an _Evidence_ during the same test. `Disclaimer:` This interaction requires an FAQ to be 100% confirmed, but as of now, it is the most probable interpretation of the rules. ___ ### **Other Cards:** **0xp Cards:** * We are using [Schoffner's Catalogue](/card/08072) because, contrary to events, it is not wasted if you discard it with [Short Supply](/card/08071) at the beginning of the game. It is also since we use [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) and if you have it in your hand, simply commit it to any test before recurring it. It also has a slightly better tempo than [Crack the Case](/card/05110), as it won't be re-shuffled in your deck once your deck is running out of cards and, therefore, won't take one draw. * Once all your items are in play, the catalogue is not useful anymore, and [Crack the Case](/card/05110) becomes better as it brings some cash to fuel [Plucky (3)](/card/08081). * Another idea is [Astounding Revelation](/card/06023), as you do not need to drop a clue to use it while searching your deck with [Captivating Discovery](/card/09047). I did not go for this one because it is "only" 2 resources. * [Cryptic Writings](/card/60215) is also available as an alternative. It can be played fast when drawn via [Captivating Discovery](/card/09047)/[Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041) and worst case scenario it commits for a . * You are free to use whichever of these cards you want to play the most, they will all do amazing. * [Resourceful](/card/03039), as explained, compensates for [Short Supply](/card/08071) that could be trashing both [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332), affecting the set-up pace tremendously. * [Deduction](/card/01039) and [Sharp Vision](/card/06204) accelerate the rate of collecting the clues you've dropped, bringing additional tempo. We upgrade later to [Deduction (2)](/card/02150) to get another tempo boost. * [Arcane Enlightenment](/card/60205) is for holding the second [Research Notes](/card/09045), so you can have 2 [Newspaper (2)](/card/03313) in play too. When you don't need it, it also commits for 2 . * [True Understanding](/card/04153) brings an added value, especially on treacheries like [Locked Door](/card/01174), since you can then discover the clue before it is discarded, thus collecting the _Evidence_ on your [Kodak](/card/09016), which would be impossible otherwise. **Higher XP Cards:** * [Hiking Boots](/card/08035) will bring good value as you will empty locations more or less every round (depending on the number of players). We prefer it over [Pathfinder](/card/02108) because it is cheaper in XP and is [Scavengeable](/card/06332) since it's an item. The is the cherry on top. * If there is one thing that I hate as a cluever, it is to spend my actions playing assets rather than investigating. No wonder [Unearth the Ancients (2)](/card/08039) is one of my favourite cards; playing your assets as you keep getting clues. * The targets are [Dr. William T. Maleson](/card/02302), [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041), [Arcane Enlightenment](/card/60205) and [Quick Study](/card/04154). * When you have all your Assets in play, it turns into a commit which is always nice. * [Plucky (3)](/card/08081) brings interesting stat boosts, a skill pump to get rid of your excessive resource, and most importantly, a very needed Horror soak for the [Ruined Film](/card/09017) and the fast cycling speed once fully set up. * [Quick Learner (4)](/card/60530) is an excellent way to spend your last bits of XP when you are done with all the rest. With 2 [Press Pass](/card/09055) in play, you will have 5 actions each turn, so 3 of them with -2 test difficulty. Spending 1 _Evidence_ with [Darrell's](/card/09015) ability almost assures you to bring any test to (0) difficulty. * Alternatively, dropping a clue with [Quick Study](/card/04154) or spending 1 _Evidence_ on your first action if you need to take a test will compensate for the downside of [Quick Learner](/card/60530). * Finally, you can use your first action to do something that doesn't involve a test, like moving, playing [Captivating Discovery](/card/09047) (which activates [Press Pass](/card/09055) for 2 additional actions with -2 difficulty), clearing someone's Weakness... * At the very end, if we do not know what to do with our XP anymore, [Versatile](/card/06167) can be a fun option. * Bring whatever makes you feel good, but I thought that [Breaking and Entering](/card/07114) was a cool card to bring. The "_add_" stat helps to compensate for the fact that you're probably using it as your first action, and so you get +2 difficulty with [Quick Learner](/card/60530), and it helps collect _Evidence_ on enemies easily! * The other cards that we can add with the 4 more slots available are [Exploit Weakness](/card/09105) and [Shed a Light](/card/09108) because the -2 difficulty during 3 turns with [Quick Learner](/card/60530) opens a lot of doors for testing at difficulty 0 by using 1 _Evidence_ with [Darrell](/card/09015)'s ability. ___ ### **Upgrade Path:** [Link to the 0xp Deck](https://arkhamdb.com/deck/view/2411051) ___ [Scavenging](/card/01073) x2 [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) x2 - 4xp - Total 4xp ___ [Arcane Enlightenment](/card/60205) x1 + [The Raven Quill](/card/09042) x1 [Press Pass](/card/09055) x2 + [Relic Hunter](/card/01695) - 7xp - Total 11xp ___ [Hawk-Eye Folding Camera](/card/05154) x2 [Newspaper (2)](/card/03313) x2 - 4xp - Total 15xp ___ [True Understanding](/card/04153) x1 [Plucky (3)](/card/08081) x1 - 3xp - Total 18xp ___ [Empirical Hypothesis](/card/09041) x1 [Unearth the Ancients (2)](/card/08039) x1 - 2xp - Total 20xp ___ [Lucky!](/card/01080) x2 [Quick Study](/card/04154) x2 - 4xp - Total 24xp ___ [Dr. William T. Maleson](/card/02302) x1 [Dr. William T. Maleson (2)](/card/09054) x1 - 2xp - Total 26xp ___ [Eureka!](/card/03231) x2 [Hiking Boots](/card/08035) x1 + [Knowledge is Power](/card/05231) x1 - 3xp - Total 29xp ___ [Quick Learner](/card/60530) x2 - 8xp - Total 37xp ___ [Deduction](/card/01039) x2 [Deduction (2)](/card/02150) x2 - 4xp - Total 41xp ___ [True Understanding](/card/04153) x1 + [Crack the Case](/card/05110) x1 [Sharp Vision](/card/06204) x2 - 2xp - Total 45xp ___ [Link to the full xp Deck](https://arkhamdb.com/deck/view/2425915) ___ ### **Make your own Deck!:** * To understand some of the archetypes and cards available to Darrell, there is [This Great Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AOJKiSPBUwI) made by Veronica from _Until the End of Time_. I will try to explain here a couple of them that I have also considered and explain the strategies of each deck very briefly. * [Here is another proposal for a Deck](https://arkhamdb.com/deck/view/2431976) that uses Darrell's test reduction to reduce the difficulty to 0. This deck is better for True Solo and Flex and is also super fun to play! * Combine the shroud reductions of [Old Keyring (3)](/card/60507) and [Flashlight (3)](/card/09122) to bring locations with a shroud of 4 and less to 0 and get an additional clue. This also allows you to play [Shed a Light](/card/09108) if there are more than 2 clues. If the shroud is 5 or more, you can add [Gumption](/card/09112) or spend an _Evidence_ from your [Darrell's Kodak](/card/09016). * For enemy management, you can use [Exploit Weakness](/card/09105) thanks to your [Flashlight (3)](/card/01087) that reduces the difficulty of evasion tests and if the enemy has 3 or more evade value you can complete with [Gumption](/card/09112) or with spending an _Evidence_. * Finally, one fun way to collect _Evidence_ on enemies with that deck is that you can evade it, then use [Belly of the Beast](/card/05160) to collect a clue and the _Evidence_ on it, and if you bring the test difficulty to 0, you can discard it during the same action! * Since the deck highly benefits from certain events, I added a lot of draw with [Practice Makes Perfect](/card/06197) to abuse [Perception (2)](/card/60228) on investigations and [Unrelenting](/card/07196) on evasions. * The weak spot of this deck is the lower _Evidence_ count. To absorb the horror of the deck reshuffling and [Ruined Film](/card/09017), we take advantage of the [Cherished Keepsake](/card/03114) and [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) combo and why not [Plucky (3)](/card/08081) later. * For a level 0 version of that deck, [here's how I would approach it](https://arkhamdb.com/deck/view/2457541). * Since [Darrell](/card/09015) can turn any test to a difficulty 0, are not an issue to him. We can imagine a [Deck like this](https://arkhamdb.com/deck/view/2426785) that would also take advantage of the cursed cards available, for instance, to heal himself with [Spirit of Humanity](/card/07229) or to draw cards with [Deep Knowledge](/card/07023). * This also allows [Token of Faith](/card/07033) to bring a steady flow of that can be used later for [A Watchful Peace](/card/07269). * There is also a basic [Scavenging (2)](/card/06332) Darrell Deck possible that simply cycles [Ice Pick (3)](/card/08107), and other than that, is relatively similar to a Minh Deck. But come on, I couldn't come back to the community with [Yet](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/36402/clue-vacuuming-rex-revised-eote-tdl-beginner-deck-guide-1.0) [An](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/37357/yorick-gold-digger-beginner-deck-guide-series-1.0)-[Other](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/37360/scavenge-minhg-beginner-deck-guide-series-1.0) [Freaking](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/36538/pete-the-underdog-revised-tdl-eote-beginner-deck-guide-1.0) [Deck](https://arkhamdb.com/decklist/view/37084/silas-mharshpoon-brutal-fighter-advanced-deck-guide-1.0) using this combo. ___ _[To create your own guides, find the template I have created here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vWi3g82i65MV8p6c4VKkYbKDT2blkim4STZ4tpugCNI/edit?usp=sharing)_
